scope_boundary: Standing per-chapter disposition record for the 66 active manifest chapter core claims. It consolidates accepted no-change transitions, accepted no-promotion decisions, Appendix C open gaps, and per-chapter evidence-plan promotion paths. It creates no support-state movement.

dispositions.chapter_title: ASI Is a Stack, Not a Model
dispositions.part_title: Part I - Foundations, Alignment, and Governance
dispositions.core_claim: Efficient ASI should be modeled as a governed stack of cooperating layers rather than as one undifferentiated model.
required_evidence: end-to-end governed stack trace
layer handoff artifacts with authority receipts
accepted transition for a narrowed stack-mechanics claim
blockers: no deployed or replayed stack run
finite proofs do not establish whole-stack behavior
dispositions.promotion_burden: The chapter has source mappings and finite stack-boundary Lean hooks, but no end-to-end stack implementation, deployed handoff trace, integration benchmark, or independent architecture review has been accepted as evidence for the broad stack claim.
non_claims: does not prove ASI is implemented
does not prove stack architecture outperforms model-centric alternatives
dispositions.current_evidence_summary: Source notes available for all 8 assigned sources; 8 exact claim-level source-note mappings recorded; support remains at the recorded state until an accepted evidence transition, proof, test, or source-derived promotion justifies movement.

dispositions.human_reader_focus: Open the book with a concrete system story instead of taxonomy.
dispositions.what_would_move_this: A state-machine proof plus a replayed handoff fixture; no broad ASI claim.

dispositions.chapter_title: The Efficient ASI Hypothesis
dispositions.part_title: Part I - Foundations, Alignment, and Governance
dispositions.core_claim: On repeated workloads with multiple authorized routes, selecting the lowest-cost route that satisfies a fixed quality predicate and compiling reusable work can improve useful-task success per total contract cost over always-maximal and always-cheapest policies, provided authority, verification, residual, and fallback obligations remain intact.
required_evidence: route-search workload suite with task contracts, quality predicates, and authority ceilings
matched baseline route records including rejected lower-cost routes
cost, quality, residual, verification, and fallback measurements
hidden human repair burden and deferred-work audit
compression or reuse utility benchmark with negative controls
independent review of source interpretation adequacy
accepted upward evidence-transition review for any narrowed stronger claim
blockers: no hidden human repair burden audit
dispositions.promotion_burden: Move above argument only after the claim is narrowed to deterministic accounting behavior that passed, or after route-search workloads, matched baselines, negative controls, cost-quality-residual measurements, hidden-cost audits, fallback traces, and independent source-interpretation review are recorded with accepted evidence-transition records.
limitations: deterministic fixtures do not run a routed ASI stack
schema validation does not prove route quality or compression utility
finite proof envelopes do not establish measured efficiency
useful-solution-per-second remains a target metric, not a measured local result
no independent model-evaluation or source-interpretation review has been recorded
non_claims: does not promote Efficient ASI above argument
does not prove route efficiency
does not prove useful-solution-per-second performance
does not prove compression or reuse utility
does not prove runtime routing or deployment behavior
dispositions.current_evidence_summary: Source notes available for all 14 assigned sources; 14 exact claim-level source-note mappings recorded; support remains at the recorded state until an accepted evidence transition, proof, test, or source-derived promotion justifies movement.
dispositions.open_gap: Review the mapped source-note support against source passages, evidence transitions, and tests before raising support state.
dispositions.primary_evidence_lane: Cost/resource harness plus external routing literature
dispositions.formal_or_prototype_work: Extend the costed-route slice beyond toy routes and tie it to budget ledgers.
dispositions.human_reader_focus: Explain efficiency as "do only the expensive work that earns its cost."
dispositions.what_would_move_this: Public fixture with baseline, negative control, and repeated run record.

synthetic fixtures do not prove deployed tool-wrapper behavior

dispositions.chapter_title: Scalable Oversight and Adversarial AI Control
dispositions.part_title: Part I - Foundations, Alignment, and Governance
dispositions.core_claim: A governed stack admits scalable oversight only as a versioned, consumer-bound protocol receipt rather than a vote: it prospectively records task, cohort, risk and authority scope; supervisor and system capability envelopes; evidence views; roles, incentives, and dependency graph; informed direct-review baseline; declared outcome-audit path; calibration, coverage, and abstention semantics; persuasion, correlation, operator-cost, and monitorability residuals; escalation owner; expiry; and requalification triggers. The receipt may inform only its permitted review or training consumer through the owning gate and cannot by itself establish reviewer independence, reliable supervision, correctness, safety, support movement, release readiness, or execution authority.
required_evidence: public-safe matched oversight workload with direct-review baseline and negative controls
independent outcome audit with correlation, calibration, cost, disagreement, and residual records
accepted transition for a narrowed oversight-admission claim
blockers: no local supervision protocol, evaluator, or outcome workload
no measured reviewer calibration, independence, or protocol comparison
finite theorems do not establish reviewer correctness, debate efficacy, weak-to-strong generalization, alignment, safety, or generality
dispositions.promotion_burden: The chapter has source-noted weak-supervision and weak-judge protocol comparators plus finite routing theorems, but no local oversight protocol, weak judge, human study, outcome audit, calibration result, training signal, or workload has been accepted as evidence for the broad claim.
non_claims: does not prove reliable supervision or evaluator independence
does not prove debate or consultancy efficacy, training safety, or execution authority
does not prove model quality, alignment, safety, or ASI
dispositions.current_evidence_summary: Source notes available for all 8 assigned sources; 8 exact claim-level source-note mappings recorded; support remains at the recorded state until an accepted evidence transition, proof, test, or source-derived promotion justifies movement.
dispositions.open_gap: Review the mapped source-note support against source passages, evidence transitions, and tests before raising support state.
dispositions.primary_evidence_lane: Passage-reviewed weak-to-strong, original debate-game, and weak-judge protocol comparators plus a finite admission boundary
dispositions.formal_or_prototype_work: Build a public-safe oversight-protocol record with task/risk/authority scope, supervisor/system envelopes, information access, role incentives, shared-dependency graph, direct-review baseline, independent outcome audit, consumer scope, expiry, residuals, escalation, and requested downstream handoff.
dispositions.human_reader_focus: Distinguish help from settlement through the selectively reported update trace; preserve what the supervisor could not check and why abstention or quarantine remains warranted.
dispositions.what_would_move_this: A replayed matched direct-review, assisted-review, correlated-failure, and abstention workload with held-out outcome reference, baseline, costs, disagreement, residuals, permitted consumers, and accountable escalation; no local supervisor calibration, debate efficacy, weak-to-strong generalization, alignment, safety, execution-authority, or ASI claim.

dispositions.chapter_title: Human Intent as a Formal Input
dispositions.part_title: Part I - Foundations, Alignment, and Governance
dispositions.core_claim: A governed stack admits human intent only as a versioned interpretation contract that preserves the raw request while separately recording the desired outcome; allowed and forbidden means; authority basis, ceiling, and affected parties; source, privacy, and publication boundaries; acceptance and evidence requirements; field provenance; confirmed assumptions, bounded defaults, contested or open ambiguities; stop, expiry, revocation, appeal, and re-contract conditions; and permitted downstream consumers. The accepted contract may bound planning only after its owning policy and authority gates admit it; it cannot by itself prove the person's complete preference, value alignment, informed consent, satisfaction, affected-party authorization, or permission for training, publication, deployment, spending, tool use, or other external effects.

ambiguous-intent negative cases
artifact satisfaction review
dispositions.promotion_burden: The chapter has exact source mappings and finite intent-contract hooks, but no parser evaluation, user-study evidence, contract compiler run, or intent-to-execution trace has been accepted for support-state promotion.
non_claims: does not prove intent capture quality
does not prove human-goal preservation

dispositions.chapter_title: Human Factors and Meaningful Control in Oversight
dispositions.part_title: Part I - Foundations, Alignment, and Governance
dispositions.core_claim: Meaningful oversight is a resource-bounded control contract: the system must preserve an identified human controller's knowledge, authority, time, observability, and effective intervention path, and must degrade or abstain when that control envelope cannot be maintained.
required_evidence: preregistered representative human-subject trials with strong allocation and interface baselines, natural and adversarial tasks, ethical stopping rules, and complete denominators
joint measurement of comprehension, correct and false intervention, timing, workload, trust calibration, useful throughput, residual harm, and responsibility allocation
independent reproduction and transfer before any broad meaningful-control support change
blockers: no local representative human-subject study or deployed oversight envelope
no independently calibrated workload, comprehension, or intervention-latency thresholds
legacy automation evidence does not by itself establish transfer to modern agentic systems
dispositions.promotion_burden: The chapter has a source-reviewed control-envelope design and human-automation comparators, but no representative ASI Stack human-subject campaign, calibrated control threshold, exercised deployed intervention system, independent reproduction, or transfer result has been accepted for its broad claim.
non_claims: does not prove operator comprehension, competence, consent, or effective intervention
does not prove that a visible human, explanation, dashboard, or approval click supplies meaningful control
does not prove safety, accountability, readiness, deployment, or ASI
dispositions.current_evidence_summary: Source notes available for all 10 assigned sources; 10 exact claim-level source-note mappings recorded; support remains at the recorded state until an accepted evidence transition, proof, test, or source-derived promotion justifies movement.
dispositions.open_gap: Review the mapped source-note support against source passages, evidence transitions, and tests before raising support state.
dispositions.primary_evidence_lane: Human-factors literature plus a resource-bounded oversight-envelope campaign
dispositions.formal_or_prototype_work: Formalize finite degradation and responsibility limits; build workload, comprehension, mode, intervention-latency, rehearsal, and safe-hold instrumentation.
dispositions.human_reader_focus: Show why a person in the loop is not meaningful control unless they can understand, decide, intervene, and recover in time.
dispositions.what_would_move_this: Representative preregistered human-subject trials beat strong interface and allocation baselines on joint detection, comprehension, correct intervention, useful throughput, workload, and residual harm without responsibility or authority laundering.

dispositions.chapter_title: Constitutional Alignment: Agency, Dignity, and Corrigibility
dispositions.part_title: Part I - Foundations, Alignment, and Governance
dispositions.core_claim: A constitutional alignment substrate should be represented as a versioned, non-self-authorizing constraint contract that binds each active predicate to its normative source and authorship process, protected scope and affected parties, operational test, precedence and conflict behavior, evidence and uncertainty, authorized interpreters and consumers, pre-effect rights and correction channels, expiry and review cadence, and migration, rollback, appeal, dissent, and residual rules. It may narrow, delay, escalate, block, or require re-contracting of separately authorized work, but it cannot grant action authority or prove moral correctness, legitimacy, dignity preservation, informed consent, reviewer independence, whole-system corrigibility, or deployed safety by itself.

reviewed predicate-translation corpus with positive and negative controls
runtime trace showing protected predicates narrow, defer, block, or route actual work
self-modification safety test over actual predicate migration and rollback behavior
least-sufficient-power evaluation over real tool, memory, planning, or self-improvement choices
independent moral, governance, runtime, formal-methods, or source-interpretation review
accepted upward evidence-transition review for any narrowed stronger claim
blockers: no deployed constitutional policy engine or constraint compiler
no runtime protected-predicate trace over real work
no self-modification migration or rollback test
no least-sufficient-power evaluation over real choices
no independent moral, governance, runtime, formal-methods, or source-interpretation review
dispositions.promotion_burden: Move above argument only after the claim is narrowed to the synthetic predicate discipline that passed, or after deployed constitutional constraint compilation, predicate-translation review, runtime protected-predicate traces, self-modification migration and rollback tests, least-sufficient-power evaluations, and independent moral, governance, runtime, formal-methods, or source-interpretation review are recorded with accepted evidence-transition records.
limitations: synthetic fixtures do not prove that real plans are constitutionally constrained
schema validation does not prove moral correctness or predicate adequacy
finite Lean predicates do not implement a constitutional policy engine
review-route strings do not prove reviewer independence or review quality
least-sufficient-power strings do not prove runtime route selection or power minimization
no independent moral, governance, runtime, formal-methods, or source-interpretation review has been recorded
non_claims: does not promote Constitutional Alignment Substrate above argument
does not prove deployed constitutional alignment
does not prove moral correctness
does not prove runtime policy behavior or self-modification safety

dispositions.chapter_title: Inner Alignment, Mesa-Optimization, and Learned-Objective Integrity
dispositions.part_title: Part I - Foundations, Alignment, and Governance
dispositions.core_claim: Consequential deployment requires a Learned-Objective Integrity Record binding the outer target, actual learning signals, model identity, behaviorally equivalent policy hypotheses, internal-optimization evidence, goal-generalization and conditional-policy tests, independent behavioral/interventional/white-box evidence, deployment opportunity, power indicators, mitigation hiding tests, monitoring, rollback, descendant invalidation, costs, residuals, and non-authorities.

independent behavioral, causal-intervention, training-lineage, and white-box methods with competence controls, complete attempted-run denominators, concealment and capability-damage measures, and explicit unresolved hypotheses
independent reproduction and transfer across a second model family without converting finite objective evidence into alignment, safety, readiness, or release authority

no independent method family or evaluator has reproduced an objective-integrity result

dispositions.chapter_title: Moral Uncertainty, Value Conflict, and Contestable Governance
dispositions.part_title: Part I - Foundations, Alignment, and Governance
dispositions.core_claim: A contestable governance layer should represent each action under unresolved value conflict as a versioned decision lease plus a linked rights receipt. The lease binds value propositions and their epistemic status, affected parties and standing, stakes and reversibility, authority and consent boundaries, the declared aggregation or precedence rule, preserved dissent, evidence and uncertainty, permitted and prohibited actions, expiry and revisit triggers, and rollback or redress. The rights receipt binds audit and explanation artifacts, independent-enough custody and review, denial and redaction reasons, appeal and correction routes, exit and export scope, safety-limited fork obligations, portability residuals, and downstream preservation. The pair may narrow or delay separately authorized action but cannot settle moral truth, manufacture consensus, grant authority, establish legal rights or legitimacy, prove material contestability, or guarantee safe exit, export, fork, replacement, self-modification, or deployed governance by itself.
required_evidence: reviewed value-conflict scenario library with positive and negative controls
classification-quality review across contested moral cases
human-review or tribunal-quality review for high-stakes conflict decisions
dissent preservation test over actual bounded decisions
runtime policy trace showing authority narrowing and revisit triggers under conflict
independent review of source interpretation adequacy
accepted upward evidence-transition review for any narrowed stronger claim
blockers: no dissent-preservation test over real decisions
dispositions.promotion_burden: Move above argument only after the claim is narrowed to the synthetic record discipline that passed, or after reviewed scenario libraries, classification-quality review, human-review or tribunal-quality review, dissent-preservation tests, runtime policy traces, and independent source-interpretation review are recorded with accepted evidence-transition records.
limitations: synthetic fixtures do not classify open-ended moral conflicts
schema validation does not prove moral correctness or stakeholder adequacy
finite Lean predicates do not implement a value-conflict policy engine
review-route text does not prove reviewer independence or review quality
bounded-decision fixtures do not prove authority narrowing in a deployed system
no independent moral, governance, or source-interpretation review has been recorded
non_claims: does not promote Moral Uncertainty above argument
does not prove moral correctness
does not prove value-conflict classification quality
does not prove reviewer independence, human-review quality, or tribunal quality
does not prove source interpretation
does not prove runtime policy behavior
dispositions.current_evidence_summary: Source notes available for all 14 assigned sources; 13 exact claim-level source-note mappings recorded; support remains at the recorded state until an accepted evidence transition, proof, test, or source-derived promotion justifies movement.
dispositions.open_gap: Review the mapped source-note support against source passages, evidence transitions, and tests before raising support state.
dispositions.primary_evidence_lane: Safety-critical Lean plus value-conflict and governance-right harnesses

dispositions.chapter_title: Stable Capability Fields
dispositions.part_title: Part I - Foundations, Alignment, and Governance
dispositions.core_claim: A Stable Capability Field should be a versioned, consumer-relative substitution contract rather than a capability name or implementation slot. It binds the field's semantic identity and observable interface—including admissible inputs, outputs, preconditions, postconditions, failures, abstentions, nondeterminism and resource bounds—to an authority ceiling, affected consumers, exact implementation and dependency identities, qualification context and lease, evaluator and evidence lineage, lifecycle and incident state, migration duties, preserved regressions, and effect-complete rollback obligations. A candidate may inherit a field route only for the declared consumer, use, environment, threat model, and epoch when independently checkable evidence shows the required refinement and no unauthorized authority expansion; otherwise it remains shadowed, canaried, quarantined, deprecated, residual, or rejected. The record does not by itself prove semantic equivalence, safe composition, evaluator independence, production safety, or successful rollback.

runtime trace showing route validity under accepted and rejected field candidates
independent evaluator-integrity or evaluator-separation review
authority-enforcement trace showing candidates cannot widen authority without governance grant
replacement transaction with real rollback dry run or rollback execution
regression suite over real candidate implementations and prior implementations
source interpretation review for the SCF source mappings
accepted upward evidence-transition review for any narrowed stronger claim
blockers: no authority-enforcement trace over real candidates
no real rollback dry run or rollback execution
no regression suite over real candidate and prior implementations
dispositions.promotion_burden: Move above argument only after the claim is narrowed to the synthetic record discipline that passed, or after a deployed stable-capability-field implementation, real route-validity traces, independent evaluator-integrity review, authority-enforcement traces, replacement transactions with real rollback dry runs or rollback execution, regression suites over real implementations, and source-interpretation review are recorded with accepted evidence-transition records.
limitations: synthetic fixtures do not prove runtime route validity
schema validation does not prove deployed capability identity
finite Lean predicates do not implement stable capability fields
evaluator-independence strings do not prove evaluator integrity
rollback-obligation strings do not prove rollback execution
no independent runtime, formal-methods, evaluator-integrity, replacement-safety, or source-interpretation review has been recorded
non_claims: does not promote Stable Capability Fields above argument
does not prove runtime route validity
does not prove deployed capability identity
does not prove evaluator integrity or authority enforcement
does not prove replacement safety or rollback execution
does not prove source interpretation

dispositions.chapter_title: Capability Replacement and Rollback
dispositions.part_title: Part I - Foundations, Alignment, and Governance
dispositions.core_claim: Capability replacement should be a prospectively authorized, phase-gated transaction over a declared Stable Capability Field, not a component swap. The transaction binds the exact prior and candidate artifacts and dependencies; field and consumer scope; change class; pre-state, checkpoint authority, state and effect inventory; qualification, regression, adversarial and transfer evidence; authority and approval; evaluator dependencies; isolation and canary exposure; monitor policy, delay and triggers; commit point; rollback, reverse-migration or compensation procedure; affected descendants and external commitments; residual owners; and terminal receipt. Default promotion is permitted only inside the evidenced scope after its declared gates pass and its recovery path is rehearsed to the stated objective. The record cannot make irreversible effects reversible, prove semantic recovery, validate its own monitor or evaluator, grant authority, establish useful improvement, or generalize inventory-exact local restoration to production.
required_evidence: deployed capability replacement implementation with real candidate and prior implementations
runtime replacement trace showing accepted, rejected, canaried, committed, and rolled-back transactions
independent evaluator-integrity or evaluator-separation review
authority-enforcement trace showing candidates cannot widen authority without governance grant
rollback dry run or rollback execution over actual implementation state
regression suite over real candidate and prior implementations
monitor-window result over real workload or replay traces
source interpretation review for replacement and rollback source mappings
accepted upward evidence-transition review for any narrowed stronger claim
blockers: no authority-enforcement trace over real candidates
no rollback dry run or rollback execution over actual implementation state
no regression suite over real candidate and prior implementations
no monitor-window result over real workload
dispositions.promotion_burden: Move above argument only after the claim is narrowed to the synthetic record discipline that passed, or after deployed replacement implementation traces, accepted/rejected/canaried/committed/rolled-back transaction replays, independent evaluator-integrity review, authority-enforcement traces, rollback dry runs or rollback execution over actual state, regression suites over real implementations, monitor-window results, and source-interpretation review are recorded with accepted evidence-transition records.
limitations: synthetic fixtures do not prove deployed replacement behavior

required_evidence: held-out partially observed intervention workload with reactive, model-free, strong single-model, ensemble, and governed baselines under matched resources
calibration, shift, disagreement, exploitation, residual, useful-outcome, unsafe-action, latency, and governance-cost records with causal ablations
independent reproduction and transfer to a second environment
blockers: no local learned world-model campaign or held-out causal intervention benchmark
no representative threshold connects prediction support and uncertainty to safe action admission
reported benchmark performance does not establish open-world grounding, causal fidelity, or transfer
dispositions.promotion_burden: The chapter has source-reviewed learned-world-model comparators and a versioned prediction-service design, but no local held-out causal intervention campaign, calibrated planning result, real-world transfer, independent reproduction, or accepted support transition exists for the broad claim.
non_claims: does not prove that a latent state, simulation, or rollout represents reality
does not prove causal prediction, calibrated planning, safe action selection, or real-world transfer
does not prove readiness, deployment authority, safety, or ASI
dispositions.current_evidence_summary: Source notes available for all 14 assigned sources; 14 exact claim-level source-note mappings recorded; support remains at the recorded state until an accepted evidence transition, proof, test, or source-derived promotion justifies movement.

dispositions.human_reader_focus: Separate imagination from reality and show how a useful simulator earns only bounded influence over a plan.
dispositions.what_would_move_this: On partially observed natural tasks, governed world-model routes outperform strong reactive, model-free, and single-model baselines on useful outcomes and calibration under shift without worse unsafe action, latency, or governance cost, then reproduce and transfer.

dispositions.chapter_title: Cognitive Compilation and Semantic IR
dispositions.part_title: Part II - Planning, Memory, Reasoning, and Execution
dispositions.core_claim: Cognitive Compilation should own a versioned, consumer- and target-relative translation contract that lowers an already accepted plan obligation through source, semantic, and target representations into a concrete artifact candidate while preserving addressable obligation, non-goal, authority, rights, assumption, source/context, evidence, resource, verifier, repair, and residual lineage. Every pass binds typed source and target semantics; declared normalization, loss, and ambiguity; preconditions and postconditions; dependencies; deterministic and nondeterministic inputs; compiler and validator identities; costs; receipts; and failure consequences. Acceptance requires post-translation validation against the actual target artifact by an independent-enough evaluator, while repair uses stable semantic identities, observed mutation sets, dependency closure, downstream rebuild, and revalidation. The compiler may block, narrow, request clarification, or residualize a lowering, but it does not reinterpret intent, choose the plan, grant authority, execute effects, self-certify semantic adequacy, or move support or release state.
required_evidence: source-plan-to-generated-artifact compiler trace
artifact-aware semantic preservation validators
measured localized-repair and direct-generation comparison
blockers: fixture is hand-authored rather than emitted by a compiler
no measured repair-locality or artifact-quality comparison
dispositions.promotion_burden: The chapter now has eight reviewed source mappings, finite obligation-preservation hooks, synthetic compilation traces, and a three-project contract-preservation fixture that rejects nine semantic-laundering mutations. Those artifacts improve record discipline but do not implement a source parser or target backend, inspect generated artifact semantics, measure repair locality, or justify promotion.
non_claims: does not prove cognitive compiler correctness
does not prove arbitrary paraphrase equivalence
does not prove artifact quality improvement or repair locality
does not reproduce the mined historical projects
dispositions.current_evidence_summary: Source notes available for all 20 assigned sources; 17 exact claim-level source-note mappings recorded; support remains at the recorded state until an accepted evidence transition, proof, test, or source-derived promotion justifies movement.
dispositions.open_gap: Review the mapped source-note support against source passages, evidence transitions, and tests before raising support state.
dispositions.primary_evidence_lane: Compiler IR fixtures plus external program-synthesis baselines
dispositions.formal_or_prototype_work: Add semantic atom lowering and failed-preservation cases.
dispositions.human_reader_focus: Use compilation as the bridge from thought to executable structure.
dispositions.what_would_move_this: IR translation preserves required fields and rejects lossy lowering.

dispositions.chapter_title: The Virtual Context ABI: Typed Pages, Cells, and Certificates
dispositions.part_title: Part II - Planning, Memory, Reasoning, and Execution
dispositions.core_claim: The Virtual Context ABI should own the static, versioned, consumer- and purpose-relative request-to-materialization contract between durable memory and model-visible context. It resolves stable object, semantic-address, version, mount, and snapshot references into finite typed representation candidates, then issues a certificate and receipt that bind exact source and field lineage, transformations, omissions and loss, provenance and taint, authority and rights, permitted and prohibited uses, freshness, lease, revocation, selection and omitted frontier, requested and observed adequacy state, costs, faults, and residuals. Admission means only that the actual packet conforms to the frozen request and policy; it does not establish truth, verification adequacy, model use, usefulness, safety, or support. The ABI may deny, ask, abstain, refresh, broaden, narrow, or return a typed fault, but it does not update durable memory, own transactions, infer execution authority, adjudicate claims, execute effects, or move support or release state.
required_evidence: VCM resolver trace with admitted, rejected, stale, and conflict-bearing context requests
context compiler or memory-store behavior test with source provenance and taint propagation
context-cell certificate checker with source-binding, authority-ceiling, loss-contract, and permitted-use failures
summary-fidelity and omission-accounting tests for lossy context packets
long-context adequacy benchmark run with distractor and position-sensitivity controls
runtime context transaction replay with deletion closure and snapshot invariants

restart/recovery and matched-baseline test of durable-store snapshot identity, generation, expiry, revocation, revalidation, selection omissions, and materialization behavior; any independent specialist review is postpublication and not a completion gate
accepted upward evidence-transition review for any narrowed stronger claim
dispositions.promotion_burden: Move above argument only after the claim is narrowed to the specific synthetic record-gate behavior that passed, or after resolver traces, context compiler behavior, durable-store correctness and restart persistence, graph-completeness limits, certificate-truthfulness checks, summary-fidelity tests, and long-context adequacy benchmarks are recorded with accepted evidence-transition records.
limitations: synthetic fixtures do not prove deployed VCM behavior
schemas validate record shape rather than resolver semantics
external context literature is not a local reproduction or context adequacy result
the hand-authored bounded snapshot does not establish store persistence, resolver correctness, or graph completeness
non_claims: does not promote Virtual Context ABI above argument
does not prove VCM resolver behavior
does not prove context compiler correctness
does not prove memory-store correctness
does not prove certificate truthfulness
does not prove summary fidelity
does not prove long-context adequacy or model verification bandwidth
dispositions.current_evidence_summary: Source notes available for all 31 assigned sources; 24 exact claim-level source-note mappings recorded; support remains at the recorded state until an accepted evidence transition, proof, test, or source-derived promotion justifies movement.
dispositions.open_gap: Review the mapped source-note support against source passages, evidence transitions, and tests before raising support state.
dispositions.primary_evidence_lane: Context admission harness, certificate fixtures, Lean hooks, and VCM source notes
dispositions.formal_or_prototype_work: Add admission, mount, capability, typed page/cell, certificate-truthfulness, authority-ceiling, loss-contract, permitted-use, and context-window negative controls.
dispositions.human_reader_focus: Make context feel like memory with permissions, receipts, and certificates that decide what memory can be trusted for.
dispositions.what_would_move_this: Admission fixture rejects stale, conflicting, mode-confused, stale-certificate, missing-certificate, or authority-escalating packets and records residual.

Commit, branch, merge, abort, retry, compaction, deletion, revocation, and recovery must preserve exact identities, obligations, faults, costs, and residuals. The transaction layer may change durable context state, but it does not own static packet materialization, semantic truth, belief revision, model/optimizer state, external effects, artifact correctness, verification adequacy, support, or release.

taint and deletion-closure negative cases

does not prove taint isolation in a deployed system

dispositions.chapter_title: Verification Bandwidth and Context Adequacy
dispositions.part_title: Part II - Planning, Memory, Reasoning, and Execution
dispositions.core_claim: Verification Bandwidth should own the prospective, claim-specific adequacy contract for a verification attempt. Before outcomes, it binds target proposition and scope, population and environment, risk and consequence, requested support effect, required positive, negative, boundary, contradiction, counterexample, and transfer obligations, available source units, verification modes, tools, evaluator-dependency graph, authority and rights, budget, horizon, stop rule, and escalation path. After execution, it records every attempted, passed, failed, disputed, unknown, infeasible, and unattempted obligation plus actual artifacts, costs, disagreement, residuals, expiry, and causal-use observations. Adequacy means only that the declared verification program was sufficient for its exact purpose under stated premises; it does not establish claim truth, model cognition, source correctness, formal-model fidelity, useful outcomes, safety, support promotion, or release.
required_evidence: context adequacy benchmark
negative controls for insufficient context
reviewed classifier or adjudication traces
dispositions.promotion_burden: The chapter has source mappings and finite adequacy hooks, but no contradiction-rate result, distractor-resistance test, adequacy classifier evaluation, or verification-bandwidth benchmark has been accepted.
non_claims: does not prove context adequacy detection
does not prove source interpretation quality

dispositions.open_gap: a workload with correct and incorrect initial candidates; adaptive benefit at a matched real budget; independently assessed evaluator and production transfer; independently assessed evaluator validity; multiple models, seeds, and natural workloads; nonzero substantive answer utility; production routing safety, latency, cost, and transfer; replicated corruption reduction
dispositions.primary_evidence_lane: Adequacy harness plus external context-eval literature
dispositions.formal_or_prototype_work: Add verifier-capacity constraints and escalation paths.
dispositions.human_reader_focus: Explain why a model cannot verify everything it can read.
dispositions.what_would_move_this: Adequacy record rejects context that exceeds verifier bandwidth.

dispositions.chapter_title: Claim Ledgers and Belief Revision
dispositions.part_title: Part II - Planning, Memory, Reasoning, and Execution
dispositions.core_claim: Claim Ledgers should own the durable identity and append-only state-transition history of each material claim and its semantic variants. Every record binds canonical proposition and scope, definitions and assumptions, population and environment, provenance and source roles, evidence and attack refs, support and uncertainty states, contradiction and defeater links, dependencies, ontology version, lifecycle, commitment, authority and rights, surface refs, expiry, residuals, and current materialized view; every proposed update binds trigger, before/after states, transition type, evidence-transition and review refs, affected dependency closure, surface-sync plan, concurrency base, migration, costs, and non-overwrite receipt. The ledger may record or route promotion, downgrade, split, merge, supersession, deprecation, retirement, dispute, or no change only through the owning gates. It does not establish claim truth, source or evidence validity, verification adequacy, semantic equivalence, reviewer competence, formal-model fidelity, action authority, usefulness, safety, support movement, or release.

contradiction detection benchmark or reviewed fixture suite with positive and negative controls

synthetic contradiction quarantine does not measure contradiction-detection quality

does not prove deployed belief revision or runtime behavior
